title: Error responses are plain text, not JSON
evidence: main.go:97,108,125,165 use http.Error() which writes text/plain bodies while success paths return JSON (Content-Type application/json set at top of each handler)
suggestion: Return a JSON error envelope (e.g. {"error": "..."}) so all responses are JSON as TASK.md requires

title: SQL not-found detected by string-comparing the error message
evidence: main.go:133,218,260 compare err.Error() == "sql: no rows in result set" instead of errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows)
suggestion: Use errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows); the string form is fragile across driver/stdlib versions

title: Empty book list serializes as JSON null instead of []
evidence: main.go:103,115 books is a nil []Book when no rows match, so GET /books encodes null rather than an empty array
suggestion: Initialize books := []Book{} so an empty result returns []

title: Author filter is an unanchored LIKE substring match
evidence: main.go:89,95 build "author LIKE ?" with "%"+author+"%", so ?author=Lee also matches 'Leeson' etc.
suggestion: If exact-author semantics are intended, use equality; otherwise document the substring behavior

title: Very low token efficiency / oversized agent transcript
evidence: scores.json token_efficiency=0.0082; _agent_stdout.log is ~355 MB, indicating heavy repetition/looping during generation
suggestion: Informational only — does not affect the delivered code; flags qwen3-coder-local run cost for cross-run comparison
